pr.title: Host `MELEE_COMPILERS_N.zip` on MEGA instead of Discord
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Moved the hosted `MELEE_COMPILERS_N.zip` dependency from a Discord CDN URL to a MEGA link across setup documentation and the Linux/Windows package build scripts. The PR updated download tooling accordingly: Linux installs and uses `megatools`/`megadl` instead of `curl`, while Windows installs `megatools` through scoop and uses `megatools dl` instead of `Invoke-WebRequest`. Motivation was explicitly that Discord hosting was no longer viable.
what_changed: Replaced the old Discord CDN compiler archive URL with `https://mega.nz/file/BU43wKxT#rVC11Rl7DPxfSn7V9Iu--8E7m7gc1gsJWtfVBbfmKwQ` in README setup instructions and both Linux and Windows Dockerfiles.
Updated `.github/README.md` PowerShell setup snippet to install `megatools`, download the archive with `megatools dl --no-progress --path`, and optionally uninstall `megatools` afterward.

Updated Linux setup to install `megatools` instead of `curl`, stream the MEGA archive with `megadl --no-progress "$MELEE_COMPILERS_URL" --path - | bsdtar -xvf- -C /tmp`, and remove `megatools` during cleanup.

Updated Windows package setup to install `megatools` via scoop, create a temporary `.zip` path using `[System.IO.Path]::GetTempFileName() -replace '\.[^.]+$','.zip'`, download with `megatools dl`, and uninstall `megatools` after extraction.
Preserved the existing compiler archive extraction flow: extract to a temporary `MELEE_COMPILERS` directory, rename/move `GC` to `mwcc_compiler`, then clean temporary files.
smart_moves: Changed both documentation and automated package build scripts in the same PR, avoiding divergence between manual setup and CI/container setup.
Replaced plain HTTP download commands with MEGA-aware tooling instead of assuming the MEGA web URL could be downloaded directly by `curl` or `Invoke-WebRequest`.
Kept Docker image size and installed package footprint down by uninstalling temporary download tools after the compiler archive was extracted.
Preserved `.zip` extension handling on Windows because `Expand-Archive` requires a zip-like filename, but simplified the path creation by generating a temp filename and replacing the extension.
Used `--no-progress` for scripted downloads, which keeps logs cleaner in automated package builds.
decomp_lessons: External binary toolchain dependencies are part of the decompilation environment; when their hosting changes, update every setup path together: README, Linux container, Windows container, and install scripts.
Do not rely on Discord CDN links for long-lived build dependencies; use a more durable host and a downloader appropriate for that host.
When replacing download infrastructure, preserve downstream archive layout assumptions such as `GC` being renamed to `mwcc_compiler`.
Temporary build tools should be installed only for setup and removed afterward where possible to keep reproducible build images smaller and cleaner.
Windows setup scripts may need explicit `.zip` filenames for archive expansion even when the downloaded content is a zip archive.
naming_conventions: The compiler archive remains named conceptually as `MELEE_COMPILERS_N.zip` in documentation.
The environment variable `MELEE_COMPILERS_URL` continues to hold the compiler archive URL in both Linux and Windows Dockerfiles.
The extracted `GC` folder is still renamed to `mwcc_compiler`, matching the existing project toolchain directory convention.
Temporary extraction directory naming continues to use `MELEE_COMPILERS`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that changed `MELEE_COMPILERS_URL` or replaced the MEGA-hosted compiler archive.
Search for remaining Discord CDN links in setup documentation or package scripts.
Check whether `megatools` availability caused later Linux or Windows build failures.
Audit manual setup instructions against Docker setup scripts for compiler archive extraction consistency.
